title: What Are Algorithms?
subject: Computer Science
objectives: Analyze algorithm efficiency with Big O notation

sections.heading: Algorithms are everywhere
sections.content:
An algorithm is a step-by-step set of instructions for solving a problem or completing a task. You follow algorithms every day without realizing it: a recipe is an algorithm for cooking, directions are an algorithm for getting somewhere, and a morning routine is an algorithm for starting your day.

In computer science, algorithms are precise instructions that a computer can follow. The key properties of a good algorithm are: it must be unambiguous (each step is clear), finite (it eventually ends), and effective (each step can actually be performed).

sections.heading: A simple algorithm
sections.content:
Algorithm: Find the largest number in a list

1. Set 'max' equal to the first element
2. For each remaining element in the list:
   a. If the current element is greater than 'max'
   b. Set 'max' equal to the current element
3. Return 'max'

For the list [3, 7, 2, 9, 4]:
• Start: max = 3
• Compare 7 > 3? Yes → max = 7
• Compare 2 > 7? No
• Compare 9 > 7? Yes → max = 9
• Compare 4 > 9? No
• Result: 9

sections.heading: Big O notation
sections.content:
Not all algorithms are equally efficient. Big O notation describes how an algorithm's running time grows as the input size increases.

• O(1) — Constant: Same time regardless of input size. Looking up an array element by index.
• O(log n) — Logarithmic: Time grows slowly. Binary search.
• O(n) — Linear: Time grows proportionally. Searching an unsorted list.
• O(n²) — Quadratic: Time grows rapidly. Comparing every pair of elements.

The algorithm to find the maximum value above is O(n) — it looks at each element once. If the list has 1000 elements, it takes about 1000 steps.

An algorithm is a finite, step-by-step procedure for solving a problem. Big O notation measures efficiency — how an algorithm's time scales with input size. Choosing the right algorithm can be the difference between a program that runs in seconds and one that takes hours.

aiContext: This lesson introduces algorithms at a high school computer science level. Students learn what algorithms are, see a simple example (finding the maximum), and are introduced to Big O notation for analyzing efficiency.
